{{Quote|Stealing from the Imperials is thirsty work…|Tuz|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
'''Tuz''' was a tall, overweight [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] who operated during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Tuz did not initially get involved in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but he was reluctantly drawn into service to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a result of pressure applied by the Rebel [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. Under the supervision of [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Wirriz]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Fortune (Human)|Fortune]], Tuz participated in undercover missions against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and supplied the Rebels with what he stole. Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Tuz helped [[Capture of the Sidral II|deliver]] some Imperial equipment from the stolen [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Sidral II]]'' to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], which [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] hoped would lead back to contractors working on the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I am Tuz, the great trader. No doubt you have heard of me?|Tuz|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
[[File:Ackbar_HS.jpg|thumb|200px|Admiral Ackbar recruited Tuz to the Rebel cause.]]
During the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Tuz operated as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trader]], taking advantage of other [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and profiting at their expense. Over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Tuz had a long and involved history with [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], a fellow Mon Calamari who became an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Though Tuz had no love for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], he did not care about the Rebellion either. However, [[1 BBY/Legends|several months before]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Tuz was contacted by Ackbar, who used his considerable leverage over the pirate to persuade him to assist the Rebel Alliance. To ensure the reluctant pirate's cooperation, Ackbar assigned the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Wirriz]] and the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Fortune (Human)|Fortune]] to accompany him. In the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] that followed, Tuz allowed the Rebels to make use of his Imperial contacts and participated in undercover operations, acting as a [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] against the Empire and supplying the Rebels with what he stole.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

After learning that the Empire was building the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] capable of destroying an entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]], the Alliance attempted to discover which contractors were involved in its construction. Shortly before the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] was completed,<ref name="XW">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref> Tuz aided in the [[Capture of the Sidral II|capture]] of the Imperial [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Sidral II]]'',<ref name="XW:OSG" /> which was carrying a cargo of the latest military hardware,<ref name="XW" /> including timers and [[fusion shell]]s.<ref name="XW:OSG" /> Hoping to analyze the equipment to learn who was building the Death Star, the Alliance dispatched the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Frazier]]'' to rendezvous with the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] and transfer its cargo. The operation was interrupted by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Immortal/Legends|Immortal]]'', but the Rebel forces were able to complete the transfer before escaping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="XW" />

Following the transfer, Tuz delivered the stolen equipment to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. After landing his small freighter aboard the Rebel [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], Tuz disembarked, accompanied by Wirriz, Fortune, and two of his [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards. Their arrival attracted the attention of three [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilots]]—[[Captain/Legends|Captains]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], [[Samuel Raider]], and [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco]]—who approached to see what they wanted. Raider recognized Tuz from some prior dealings and was suspicious of the pirate, but Tuz accepted Naeco's invitation to join the Rebels for a drink in the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]' lounge. While they drank, Raider voiced his opinion of Tuz's business practices, causing one of the pirate's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] to attack the pilot. Raider managed to knock the bodyguard to the floor with a well-placed uppercut, but Tuz brought the altercation to a halt before his second guard could intervene, threatening to send his guards back to the [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. The pirate admitted his checkered past and claimed that he had changed his ways, but Fortune, on Wirriz's order, told the pilots the truth about Tuz's reluctant involvement. The group finished their drinks, and the Rebels escorted Tuz and his companions back to their ship.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

Part of the stolen shipment had passed through an Imperial repair dock near [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] decided to launch an [[Attack on the Imperial repair dock|attack]] on the facility in the hopes of crippling the infrastructure that was servicing ships working on the Death Star.<ref name="XW" /> Before leaving the ''Independence'', Tuz provided some information on the facility to Commanders [[H. S'man|S'man]] and [[Hamo Blastwell]] to assist in planning such a raid.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I wouldn't trust that Calamarian for an instant. He's as treacherous as they come.|Samuel Raider|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
Operating as a pirate, Tuz would happily take advantage of any young and gullible traders he encountered to achieve profit at their expense. As a result he acquired a reputation as a swindler and a cheat. He had little interest in the Rebellion until Admiral Ackbar used his influence to persuade Tuz to work against the Empire. Never truly trusted by the Rebels, Tuz was forced to conduct his work under the supervision of Wirriz and Fortune. Tuz later claimed that he had no love for the Empire and had come to admire the commitment and dedication of the Rebels. Those within the Alliance, however, continued to doubt his claims. Despite working under the charge of the Rebels, Tuz maintained the impression that he was in command. A tall, obese Mon Calamari male whose face was scarred in three places, he typically wore a military issue tunic, covered with numerous [[commendation]]s and battle patches, a long [[Cape/Legends|cape]], and an iridescent headband. While aboard the ''Independence'', he spoke on behalf of his group and made sure he was heard complaining loudly about cheap Alliance negotiators not paying him a fair price for his cargo. On prompting by Wirriz, however, Tuz backed down and allowed Fortune to take command of the situation in the pilot's lounge.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tuz was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for the [[1993]] book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''. The book, which included strategies for completing the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], was presented as the story of Keyan Farlander, the player character in the game. Tuz makes a brief appearance in the story portion of the book, which links him to the capture of the ''Sidral II'' in the mission "Guard Weapons Transfer" and credits him with the intelligence leading to the mission "Destroy Repair Dock."<ref name="XW:OSG" />
